transcript.prompt:
You are a fresh-context agent in a coordination-benchmark task. Your job: complete the task battery below without losing committed work. You may inspect the workspace freely. Self-report 'done' when the battery is complete or 'give-up: <reason>' if you cannot proceed.

## Substrate convention

# maw arm crib (current v2 `ws/` layout)

You coordinate multi-agent work using **maw**, a workspace-management tool built on git. The repo is laid out as a bare-style root containing `.git/` and a `ws/` directory; every workspace is an isolated git worktree under `ws/<name>/`. The default integration target lives at `ws/default/`. The `maw` binary is on `$PATH`.

## Core verbs

- `maw ws create <name>` — make a new workspace (worktree) at `ws/<name>/`.
- `maw ws list` — list all workspaces with their state (active / stale / conflicted).
- `maw ws sync` — bring the current workspace up to the latest epoch (rebases workspace commits onto integration head). Use when you see `stale` in `maw ws list`.
- `maw ws diff <name>` — show the workspace's changes vs the epoch base.
- `maw ws merge <a> [b ...] --into default --check` — dry-run a merge; reports conflicts without changing state.
- `maw ws merge <a> [b ...] --into default --destroy` — merge workspace(s) into default and destroy the source(s) on success.
- `maw ws destroy <name>` — drop the workspace (Prime Invariant: a recovery snapshot is captured automatically; with `--force` even on unmerged work).
- `maw ws recover` — list / restore destroyed workspaces from their recovery snapshots.
- `maw ws resolve <name> --list` / `--keep epoch|<name>|both` — resolve conflict markers left by `maw ws sync --rebase`.
- `maw doctor` — substrate-health probe.

## Running commands inside a workspace

The sandbox does not persist `cd` between tool calls. Use `maw exec <workspace> -- <cmd>` to run any command inside a workspace:

```
maw exec alice -- git status
maw exec alice -- git add -A
maw exec alice -- git commit -m "feat: …"
```

## Conflicts are data, not errors

- `maw ws sync --rebase` does not abort on conflict — it commits the marker-laden file, records structured conflict metadata under `.manifold/`, and continues. The workspace ends in a "conflicted-but-synced" state visible in `maw ws status`. Resolve via `maw ws resolve`.
- `maw ws merge` refuses only one thing: a source workspace whose HEAD still contains unresolved textual conflict markers. Pass `--force` only when markers are legitimate content (test fixtures, docs).

## Prime Invariant

No committed work is ever lost. `maw ws destroy` always captures a recovery snapshot under `refs/manifold/recovery/<name>/`; `maw ws recover` can list, inspect, search, and restore the contents of any destroyed workspace. If you suspect work was lost, run `maw ws recover` before reopening the task.

## What to read first

- This crib (always).
- `maw --help` and `maw ws --help` for the full surface.
- `maw doctor` if anything looks broken before you start.

## Workspace

Work under: /tmp/.tmpTxHqP1
Scenario seed: 16508765558721037427

## Task battery

Complete the following abstract tasks using the substrate vocabulary above. Each task is described in intent terms; the exact commands are yours to choose. Aim to leave the substrate coherent (no orphaned state, no lost work).

1. Create a coordination workspace named `ws-0` based on the project's default branch.
2. In workspace `ws-0`, edit the following files (any content is fine; the test cares about coordination): shared/file-2.txt, ws-0/file-0.txt
3. Remove workspace `ws-0` (force even if it has uncommitted work).
4. Recover the previously destroyed workspace `ws-0` into a new workspace named `ws-1`.
transcript.convention_text:
# maw arm crib (current v2 `ws/` layout)

You coordinate multi-agent work using **maw**, a workspace-management tool built on git. The repo is laid out as a bare-style root containing `.git/` and a `ws/` directory; every workspace is an isolated git worktree under `ws/<name>/`. The default integration target lives at `ws/default/`. The `maw` binary is on `$PATH`.

## Core verbs

- `maw ws create <name>` — make a new workspace (worktree) at `ws/<name>/`.
- `maw ws list` — list all workspaces with their state (active / stale / conflicted).

- `maw ws diff <name>` — show the workspace's changes vs the epoch base.
- `maw ws merge <a> [b ...] --into default --check` — dry-run a merge; reports conflicts without changing state.
- `maw ws merge <a> [b ...] --into default --destroy` — merge workspace(s) into default and destroy the source(s) on success.
- `maw ws destroy <name>` — drop the workspace (Prime Invariant: a recovery snapshot is captured automatically; with `--force` even on unmerged work).
- `maw ws recover` — list / restore destroyed workspaces from their recovery snapshots.
- `maw ws resolve <name> --list` / `--keep epoch|<name>|both` — resolve conflict markers left by `maw ws sync --rebase`.
- `maw doctor` — substrate-health probe.

## Running commands inside a workspace

The sandbox does not persist `cd` between tool calls. Use `maw exec <workspace> -- <cmd>` to run any command inside a workspace:

```
maw exec alice -- git status
maw exec alice -- git add -A
maw exec alice -- git commit -m "feat: …"
```

## Conflicts are data, not errors

- `maw ws sync --rebase` does not abort on conflict — it commits the marker-laden file, records structured conflict metadata under `.manifold/`, and continues. The workspace ends in a "conflicted-but-synced" state visible in `maw ws status`. Resolve via `maw ws resolve`.
- `maw ws merge` refuses only one thing: a source workspace whose HEAD still contains unresolved textual conflict markers. Pass `--force` only when markers are legitimate content (test fixtures, docs).

## Prime Invariant

No committed work is ever lost. `maw ws destroy` always captures a recovery snapshot under `refs/manifold/recovery/<name>/`; `maw ws recover` can list, inspect, search, and restore the contents of any destroyed workspace. If you suspect work was lost, run `maw ws recover` before reopening the task.

## What to read first

- This crib (always).

- `maw doctor` if anything looks broken before you start.

turns.reply_text: I'll help you complete this coordination benchmark task. Let me start by understanding the current state of the workspace and then work through each step systematically.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.result_truncated:
/tmp/.tmpTxHqP1
total 4
drwxr-xr-x    5 bob  bob     120 May 28 00:39 .
drwxrwxrwt 2909 root root 547660 May 28 00:40 ..
-rw-r--r--    1 bob  bob      17 May 28 00:39 .git
drwxr-xr-x    4 bob  bob     100 May 28 00:39 .manifold
drwxr-xr-x    8 bob  bob     240 May 28 00:39 repo.git
drwxr-xr-x    3 bob  bob      60 May 28 00:39 ws

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.result_truncated:
maw doctor
==========

[OK] git: git version 2.54.0

turns.reply_text:
Good, the substrate is healthy. Now I'll work through the tasks systematically.

**Task 1: Create workspace ws-0**

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Exit code 1
Error: Workspace create requires an explicit source.
  Use one of:
    maw ws create --from main ws-0
    maw ws create --change <change-id> ws-0

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw ws create --from main ws-0","description":"Create workspace ws-0 from main branch"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Creating workspace 'ws-0' at ws/ws-0 ...

Workspace 'ws-0' ready!

  Mode:   ephemeral
  Epoch:  dc48685eaa00 (base commit for this workspace)
  Path:   /tmp/.tmpTxHqP1/ws/ws-0/

  IMPORTANT: All file reads, writes, and edits must use this path.
  This is your working directory for ALL operations, not just bash.

To start working:

  # Edit files under /tmp/.tmpTxHqP1/ws/ws-0/
  # Changes are detected automatically by the merge engine

  # Run commands in the workspace:
  maw exec ws-0 -- cargo test
  maw ws merge ws-0 --into default --destroy

Note: All edits in the workspace are tracked automatically.
The merge engine captures changes when merging.

turns.reply_text:
**Task 2: Edit files in workspace ws-0**

Let me first check what files exist in the workspace:

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- find . -type f | head -20","description":"List files in ws-0"}

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.result_truncated:
total 8
drwxr-xr-x 2 bob bob 80 May 28 00:39 .
drwxr-xr-x 4 bob bob 80 May 28 00:40 ..
-rw-r--r-- 1 bob bob 51 May 28 00:39 .git
-rw-r--r-- 1 bob bob 11 May 28 00:39 README.md

turns.reply_text: Now I'll edit the required files. First, I need to create the shared/file-2.txt file:

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- mkdir -p shared","description":"Create shared directory"}

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- echo \"edited in ws-0\" > shared/file-2.txt","description":"Edit shared/file-2.txt"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Exit code 1
(eval):1: no such file or directory: shared/file-2.txt

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- bash -c 'echo \"edited in ws-0\" > shared/file-2.txt'","description":"Edit shared/file-2.txt with bash"}

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- bash -c 'echo \"file-0 content\" > ws-0/file-0.txt'","description":"Edit ws-0/file-0.txt"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Exit code 1
bash: line 1: ws-0/file-0.txt: No such file or directory

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- mkdir -p ws-0 && maw exec ws-0 -- bash -c 'echo \"file-0 content\" > ws-0/file-0.txt'","description":"Create ws-0 directory and edit file-0.txt"}

turns.reply_text: Let me verify the edits were successful:

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- cat shared/file-2.txt && maw exec ws-0 -- cat ws-0/file-0.txt","description":"Verify file contents"}

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw ws destroy ws-0 --force","description":"Force destroy workspace ws-0"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Snapshot saved: ba50a4f95119
  State: abandoned-with-snapshot (lifecycle vocabulary, bn-29fi).
  Recover (inspect):       maw ws recover ws-0
  Recover + merge (full):  maw ws recover ws-0 --to ws-0-restored && maw ws merge ws-0-restored --into default --destroy
Workspace 'ws-0' destroyed.
RECOVERY_SURFACE for 'ws-0':
  result:       success
  commit:       no
  snapshot_ref: refs/manifold/recovery/ws-0/2026-05-28T04-40-32.417658769Z
  snapshot_oid: ba50a4f95119cd9f6cf485766c035540390ac90e
  capture_mode: worktree-snapshot
  artifact:     /tmp/.tmpTxHqP1/.manifold/artifacts/ws/ws-0/destroy/2026-05-28T04-40-32.421537596Z.json
  recover_cmd:  maw ws recover ws-0

turns.reply_text: **Task 4: Recover workspace ws-0 into a new workspace named ws-1**

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw ws recover ws-0 --to ws-1","description":"Recover destroyed workspace ws-0 as ws-1"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
[AUDIT] {"event_type":"restore","ref_name":"(workspace:ws-0)","new_workspace":"ws-1"}
Creating workspace 'ws-1' from snapshot of 'ws-0'...
Creating workspace 'ws-1' at ws/ws-1 ...

Workspace 'ws-1' ready!

  Mode:   ephemeral
  Epoch:  dc48685eaa00 (base commit for this workspace)
  Path:   /tmp/.tmpTxHqP1/ws/ws-1/

  IMPORTANT: All file reads, writes, and edits must use this path.
  This is your working directory for ALL operations, not just bash.

To start working:

  # Edit files under /tmp/.tmpTxHqP1/ws/ws-1/
  # Changes are detected automatically by the merge engine

  # Run commands in the workspace:
  maw exec ws-1 -- cargo test
  maw ws merge ws-1 --into default --destroy

Note: All edits in the workspace are tracked automatically.
The merge engine captures changes when merging.

Restored snapshot of 'ws-0' into workspace 'ws-1'.

  Snapshot:  ba50a4f95119...
  Path:      /tmp/.tmpTxHqP1/ws/ws-1/
  Recovered: 2 dirty file(s)

Next: maw exec ws-1 -- git status
      maw exec ws-1 -- git diff

turns.reply_text: Let me verify that the recovered workspace contains the edited files:

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-1 -- cat shared/file-2.txt && maw exec ws-1 -- cat ws-0/file-0.txt","description":"Verify recovered files in ws-1"}

turns.reply_text: Perfect! Let me verify the final state of all workspaces:

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.result_truncated:
default	/tmp/.tmpTxHqP1/ws/default
ws-1	/tmp/.tmpTxHqP1/ws/ws-1 (ready to merge) [lifecycle:committed-unintegrated]

Merge ready: maw ws merge ws-1 --into default --destroy

turns.reply_text:
**done**

All four tasks completed successfully:

1. ✅ **Created workspace ws-0** from main branch using `maw ws create --from main ws-0`
2. ✅ **Edited files in ws-0**:
   - Created and edited `shared/file-2.txt` with content
   - Created and edited `ws-0/file-0.txt` with content
3. ✅ **Removed workspace ws-0** with `maw ws destroy ws-0 --force`, which automatically captured a recovery snapshot
4. ✅ **Recovered workspace** from the destroyed ws-0 snapshot into new workspace ws-1 using `maw ws recover ws-0 --to ws-1`

The recovered workspace ws-1 contains all the edited files from ws-0, and the substrate remains coherent with no lost work. The recovery system successfully preserved the uncommitted changes and restored them into the new workspace.
